response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a strong 15+ year career in software engineering with genuine breadth across systems, web, and infrastructure, and his current role at re:cinq does touch on MCP servers and agentic workflows. However, the core requirements for this role expose significant gaps: Python is absent from his skill set entirely, there is no mention of vector databases or RAG architectures, and his AI-specific experience appears to have started only in April 2025. The job mandates 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, which the resume does not support. His background skews toward general full-stack and DevOps work rather than applied GenAI or enterprise AI delivery.
strengths: Active hands-on work with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and agentic workflow infrastructure in current role
Demonstrated TypeScript and Node.js depth across multiple production environments including high-throughput data integration at Dunnhumby
Staff-level engineering experience with evidence of technical mentorship, community education (workshops, talks), and open-source leadership
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume skills list — a critical gap for a GenAI-focused role where Python is the dominant ecosystem language
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or any LLM-specific tooling beyond MCP
The 8+ years of AI solution delivery requirement is not met — AI work appears to begin with the current role in April 2025, making the effective AI experience under 2 years

key_factors.factor: No RAG or vector DB experience
